data.content:
The build confirms the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to the `Compile` items within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild needs to know about the file through the `@(Compile)` items for it to be passed to the C# compiler.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The generated file must be added to the `Compile` item group so the compiler includes it. I added:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

inside the target, after the file is written.
